statement: Every countable graph has an unfriendly partition (each vertex has at least as many neighbors across as on its side).

title: Zarankiewicz problem
statement: Determine the maximum edges in a bipartite graph avoiding K_{s,t} as subgraph.

title: Suslin's problem
statement: Is every complete dense linear order without endpoints satisfying CCC isomorphic to the reals?

title: Kummer-Vandiver conjecture
statement: The p-part of class number of cyclotomic fields Q(zeta_p) is trivial for regular primes.

title: Hardy-Littlewood first conjecture
statement: The prime k-tuples conjecture predicting when patterns of primes repeat infinitely.

title: Lander-Parkin-Selfridge conjecture
statement: Counterexamples to Euler's sum of powers conjecture require at least as many terms on the right as the power.

title: Giuga conjecture
statement: p is prime iff sum of k^{p-1} for k=1..p-1 is -1 mod p.

title: Brocard's problem
statement: The only solutions to n! + 1 = m^2 are n = 4, 5, 7.

title: Erdős-Kac theorem extensions
statement: Extend the Gaussian distribution of prime factors to other arithmetic functions.

title: Wall-Sun-Sun prime conjecture
statement: Are there infinitely many Wall-Sun-Sun primes?

title: Wieferich prime conjecture
statement: Are there infinitely many Wieferich primes?

title: Mersenne prime conjecture
statement: Are there infinitely many Mersenne primes?

title: Sophie Germain prime conjecture
statement: Are there infinitely many Sophie Germain primes?

title: Safe prime conjecture
statement: Are there infinitely many safe primes?

title: Andrews-Curtis conjecture (topology)
statement: Every balanced presentation of the trivial group can be reduced via Andrews-Curtis moves.

title: Zeeman conjecture
statement: If K is a contractible 2-complex, then K x I is collapsible.

title: Dixmier conjecture
statement: Every endomorphism of the Weyl algebra A_n is an automorphism.

title: Fujita conjecture
statement: For ample line bundle L on n-dim variety, K + (n+1)L is basepoint-free and K + (n+2)L is very ample.

title: Vaught's conjecture
statement: A countable first-order theory has either countably many or continuum-many models.

title: Collatz conjecture undecidability
statement: Is the generalized Collatz problem algorithmically undecidable?

title: 196-algorithm (Lychrel numbers)
statement: Does the reverse-and-add process starting from 196 ever produce a palindrome?

title: Solitary number problem
statement: Is 10 a solitary number (is there another integer with the same abundancy index)?

title: Euler-Mascheroni constant irrationality
statement: Is the Euler-Mascheroni constant gamma irrational?

title: Odd perfect numbers
statement: Do odd perfect numbers exist?
notes: Ancient problem, no odd perfect number found

title: Cubic number representation
statement: Can every integer be written as a sum of three cubes?

title: Square site percolation threshold
statement: Determine the exact critical probability for site percolation on the square lattice.

title: Normality of pi
statement: Are the digits of pi uniformly distributed in every base?

title: Normality of e
statement: Is the base-10 expansion of e normal?

title: Normality of sqrt(2)
statement: Is the base-10 expansion of sqrt(2) normal?

title: e + pi irrationality
statement: Is e + pi irrational?

title: Infinitely many perfect numbers
statement: Are there infinitely many perfect numbers?

title: Infinitely many Fermat primes
statement: Are there infinitely many Fermat primes (primes of the form 2^{2^n}+1)?

title: Infinitely many amicable pairs
statement: Are there infinitely many amicable number pairs?

title: Existence of odd weird numbers
statement: Does an odd weird number exist?

title: Catalan's conjecture generalization
statement: Characterize all perfect powers that differ by 1 (beyond Mihailescu's theorem for prime powers).

title: Benford's law for primes
statement: Do primes satisfy Benford's law in a suitable asymptotic sense?

title: Mirror symmetry
statement: Establish the mathematical equivalence between geometrically distinct Calabi-Yau manifolds.

title: Quantum confinement and mass gap
statement: Quantitatively understand quark and gluon confinement in QCD.

title: Hales-Jewett theorem bounds
statement: Determine the exact Hales-Jewett numbers HJ(r,k).

title: Graham's number context
statement: Determine the exact value of N* in the Ramsey-type problem on hypercube colorings.

title: Frankl's conjecture (union-closed)
statement: In any finite union-closed family of sets, some element belongs to at least half the sets.

title: Rota's conjecture (matroids)
statement: Finitely many excluded minors characterize representability over any finite field.

title: Welsh's conjecture
statement: The number of bases of a matroid is log-concave.

title: Carleson's theorem extensions
statement: Does the Fourier series of every L^p function converge almost everywhere for p > 1?
notes: Proved for p>=2 by Carleson, p>1 by Hunt; open for endpoint

title: Regularity of 3D Euler equations
statement: Do smooth solutions to the incompressible 3D Euler equations develop singularities in finite time?

title: Kadison-Singer problem
statement: Every pure state on B(l^2) is uniquely determined by its restriction to a maximal abelian subalgebra.

title: Unknotting number conjecture
statement: Is the unknotting number of a knot equal to its 4-genus?

title: Slice-ribbon conjecture
statement: Is every slice knot a ribbon knot?

title: Jones polynomial detects unknot
statement: Does the Jones polynomial distinguish the unknot from all other knots?

title: Vassiliev conjecture
statement: Do Vassiliev invariants distinguish all knots?

title: Volume conjecture
statement: The colored Jones polynomial determines the hyperbolic volume of the knot complement.

title: Homotopy groups of spheres
statement: Determine the homotopy groups pi_n(S^k) for all n and k.

title: Telescope conjecture
statement: Is the telescope conjecture true in chromatic homotopy theory?

title: Homotopy hypothesis
statement: Are infinity-groupoids equivalent to topological spaces?

title: Kadison's similarity problem
statement: Is every bounded representation of a C*-algebra similar to a *-representation?

title: Naimark's problem
statement: If a C*-algebra has only one irreducible representation up to unitary equivalence, is it isomorphic to K(H)?

title: Large cardinal consistency
statement: Are there models of ZFC with specific large cardinals (measurable, supercompact, etc.)?

title: Ultimate L
statement: Does Woodin's Ultimate L conjecture hold, resolving the structure of the set-theoretic universe?

title: Proper forcing axiom consequences
statement: What are the complete consequences of PFA for the structure of the continuum?

title: Bing-Borsuk conjecture
statement: Is every n-dimensional homogeneous ANR a topological manifold?

title: Büchi's problem
statement: Does there exist a positive integer M such that every sequence of M consecutive squares has no non-trivial common difference?

title: Burnside problem (general)
statement: Is every finitely generated group of finite exponent necessarily finite?

title: Discrepancy of hypergraphs
statement: Determine tight bounds for the discrepancy of set systems.

title: Dixmier problem
statement: Is every non-commutative C*-algebra with only one irreducible representation isomorphic to compact operators?

title: Generalized Riemann hypothesis
statement: All non-trivial zeros of Dirichlet L-functions have real part 1/2.

title: Grand Riemann hypothesis
statement: All automorphic L-functions have non-trivial zeros only on the critical line.

title: Hilbert-Polya conjecture
statement: The Riemann hypothesis follows from the existence of a self-adjoint operator whose eigenvalues are the zeta zeros.

title: Kurosh problem
statement: Is every algebraic algebra over a field locally finite?

title: Magic square of squares
statement: Does a 3x3 magic square exist using nine distinct perfect squares?

title: Matrix mortality problem
statement: Is it decidable whether the zero matrix is in the semigroup generated by a given set of matrices?

title: Mutually unbiased bases
statement: Do d+1 mutually unbiased bases exist in all dimensions d?

title: Nearby Lagrangian conjecture
statement: Every closed exact Lagrangian submanifold of a cotangent bundle is Hamiltonian isotopic to the zero section.

title: Prouhet-Tarry-Escott problem
statement: Find ideal solutions to the system of equations where sums of k-th powers match.

title: Snake-in-the-box problem
statement: Determine the longest induced path in a hypercube graph.

title: Tarski's exponential function problem
statement: Is the theory of the real field with exponentiation decidable?

title: Thomson problem
statement: Determine the minimum energy configuration of N electrons on a sphere.

title: Thurston's 24 questions
statement: Collection of 24 problems in 3-manifold topology and geometry; several still open.

title: Jacobi bound problem
statement: Does the Jacobi bound hold for the order of a system of differential equations?

title: M/G/k queue
statement: Determine the exact waiting time distribution for the multi-server queue with general service times.

title: Eternity II puzzle
statement: Find a solution to the 256-piece edge-matching puzzle.

title: Borel conjecture (topology)
statement: Aspherical closed manifolds are topologically rigid: homotopy equivalent implies homeomorphic.

title: Cannon conjecture
statement: Every Gromov hyperbolic group with 2-sphere boundary acts geometrically on hyperbolic 3-space.

title: Wall conjecture
statement: Every finitely dominated connected CW complex is homotopy equivalent to a finite CW complex.

title: Baum-Connes with coefficients
statement: The assembly map with coefficients is an isomorphism for all groups and all coefficient algebras.

title: Ankeny-Artin-Chowla conjecture
statement: For primes p = 1 mod 4, the fundamental unit of Q(sqrt(p)) has specific divisibility properties.

title: Cramér-Granville conjecture
statement: Refines Cramér's conjecture on prime gaps using Maier's theorem.

title: Density hypothesis (Riemann zeta)
statement: N(sigma, T) << T^{2(1-sigma)+epsilon} for the zero-density of the Riemann zeta.

title: Guy's unsolved problems: friendly numbers
statement: Are there odd amicable pairs? Do all amicable pairs share a common factor?

title: Ben Green Problem 2
statement: Let A ⊂Z be a set of n integers. Is there a set S ⊂A of size (log n)100 with S ˆ+S disjoint from A?

title: Ben Green Problem 3
statement: Suppose that A ⊂[0, 1] is open and has measure greater than 1 3. Is there a solution to xy = z?

title: Ben Green Problem 5
statement: Which finite groups have the smallest biggest product-free sets?

title: Ben Green Problem 6
statement: Fix an integer d. What is the largest sum-free subset of [N]d?

title: Ben Green Problem 7
statement: Define Ulam’s sequence 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 11, 13, 16, 18, 26, 28, 36, . . . as fol- lows: u1 = 1, u2 = 2, and un+1 is the smallest number expressible as a sum ui +uj, i < j ⩽n, in a unique way. Does this sequence have positive density? Can one explain the curious Fourier properties of this sequence?

title: Ben Green Problem 8
statement: Suppose that A ⊂[N] has no more than εN 2 solutions to x + y = z. It is known [144] that one may remove ε′N elements of A to leave a sum-free set, where ε′ →0 as ε →0. But is there a bound for ε′ in terms of ε which is at least vaguely reasonable?

title: Ben Green Problem 9
statement: Problems about progressions. (i) (Solved) Is r3(N) ≪N(log N)−10? (ii) Is r5(N) ≪N(log N)−c? (iii) Is r4(Fn 5) ≪N 1−c, where N = 5n?

title: Ben Green Problem 10
statement: Let S ⊂N be random. Under what conditions is Roth’s theorem for progressions of length 3 true with common differences in S?

title: Ben Green Problem 12
statement: Let G be an abelian group of size N, and suppose that A ⊂G has density α. Are there at least α15N 10 tuples (x1, . . . , x5, y1, . . . , y5) ∈G10 such that xi + yj ∈A whenever j ∈{i, i + 1, i + 2}?

title: Ben Green Problem 13
statement: Suppose that A ⊂Z/NZ has density α and is Fourier uniform (that is, all Fourier coefficients of 1A −α are o(N)). Does A contain at least ≫α100N 2 4-term arithmetic progressions?

statement: Does there exist a Lipschitz function f : N →Z whose graph Γ = {(n, f(n)) : n ∈Z} ⊂Z2 is free of 3-term progressions?

title: Ben Green Problem 16
statement: What is the largest subset of [N] with no solution to x+3y = 2z+2w in distinct integers x, y, z, w?

title: Ben Green Problem 17
statement: Suppose that A ⊂Fn 3 is a set of density α. Under what conditions on α is A guaranteed to contain a 3-term progression with nonzero common difference in {0, 1}n? For fixed α, must A contain ≫α 6n such progressions?

title: Ben Green Problem 18
statement: Suppose that G is a finite group, and let A ⊂G × G be a subset of density α. Is it true that there are ≫α |G|3 triples x, y, g such that (x, y), (gx, y), (x, gy) all lie in A?

statement: Find reasonable bounds for instances of the multidimensional Sze- merédi theorem.

statement: Suppose that a1, . . . , ak are integers which do not satisfy Rado’s condition: thus if P i∈I ai = 0 then I = ∅. It then follows from Rado’s theorem that the equation a1x1 + · · · + akxk is not partition regular. Write c(a1, . . . , ak) for the least number of colours required in order to colour ...

statement: If {1, . . . , N} is r-coloured then, for N ⩾N0(r), there are integers x, y ⩾3 such that x + y, xy have the same colour. Find reasonable bounds for N0(r).

title: Ben Green Problem 24
statement: If A is a set of n integers, what is the maximum number of affine translates of the set {0, 1, 3} that can A contain?

title: Ben Green Problem 27
statement: What is the size of the smallest set A ⊂Z/pZ (with at least two elements) for which no element in the sumset A + A has a unique representation?

title: Ben Green Problem 28

title: Ben Green Problem 37
statement: What is the smallest subset of N containing, for each d = 1, . . . , N, an arithmetic progression of length k with common difference d?

title: Ben Green Problem 38
statement: What is the largest subset A ⊂Fn 7 for which A −A intersects {−1, 0, 1}n only at 0?

title: Ben Green Problem 39
statement: If A ⊂Z/pZ is random, |A| = √p, can we almost surely cover Z/pZ with 100√p translates of A?

statement: Let p be an odd prime and suppose that f : Fn p × Fn p →C is a function bounded pointwise by 1. Suppose that Eh∥∆(h,h)f∥4 □⩾δ. Does f correlate with a function of the form a(x)b(y)c(x + y)(−1)q(x,y)?

statement: Let G be an abelian group, and consider the space Φ(G) of all functions on G which are convex combinations of functions of the form ϕ(g) := Ex1+x2+x3=gf1(x2, x3)f2(x1, x3)f3(x1, x2) with ∥fi∥∞⩽1. Let Φ′(G) be the space of functions defined similarly, but with f3(x1, x2) now required to be a funct...

title: Ben Green Problem 58
statement: Suppose that A, B ⊂{1, . . . , N} both have size N 0.49. Does A + B contain a composite number?

title: Ben Green Problem 59
statement: Is every n ⩽N the sum of two integers, all of whose prime factors are at most N ε?

title: Ben Green Problem 60
statement: Is there an absolute constant c > 0 such that, if A ⊂N is a set of squares of size at least 2, then |A + A| ⩾|A|1+c?

title: Ben Green Problem 79

title: Ben Green Problem 81

title: Ben Green Problem 87
statement: Let p(k) be the limit as n →∞of the probability that a random permutation on [n] preserves some set of size k. Is p(k) a decreasing function of k? Is p(k) = (C + o(1))k−α(log k)−3/2 for some absolute constant C?

title: Ben Green Problem 88
statement: Consider a set S ⊂[N]3 with the property that any two distinct elements s, s′ of S are comparable, which means that s −s′ has either two (strictly) positive indices or two (strictly) negative ones. Is |S| ⩽N 2−δ for some δ > 0?

title: Ben Green Problem 89
statement: Let A ⊂Fn 2. If V is a subspace of Fn 2, write α(V ) for the density of A on V . Is there some V of moderately small codimension on which α is stable in the sense that |α(V ) −α(V ′)| ⩽ε whenever V ′ is a codimension 1 subspace of V ?

title: Ben Green Problem 90
statement: Suppose that A ⊂Z/pZ is a set of density 1 2. Under what conditions on K can A be almost invariant under all maps ϕ(x) = ax + b, |a|, |b| ⩽K?

title: Ben Green Problem 92
statement: I have a string x ∈{0, 1}n. An instance of ˜x is called a ‘trace’. ...

statement: Is every set Λ ⊂Z either a Sidon set, or a set of analyticity?

title: Ben Green Problem 97
statement: In how many ways (asymptotically) Q(n) may n non-attacking queens be placed on an n × n chessboard?

title: Ben Green Problem 99
statement: As Problem 98 illustrates, finding a single solution to a polynomial equation F(x1, . . . , xn) = C (say) can be very difficult, and asymptotically enu- merating such solutions inside (say) a box [X]n is still harder. Nonetheless, one may ask about going yet further, and pose the question of esti...

title: Ben Green Problem 100
statement: Is every group well-approximated by finite groups?

title: Matrix Spencer conjecture
statement: Does Spencer's discrepancy bound O(sqrt(n)) extend to matrix-valued settings with operator norm?

title: Group Spencer conjecture

statement: Which regular graphs have the property that all coupled oscillators synchronize for all initial conditions?

statement: What is the typical Lovasz theta number of an Erdos-Renyi random graph G(n,1/2)?

title: Phase retrieval injectivity
statement: What is the minimal number of measurements needed for injective phase retrieval in C^n?

title: Mutually unbiased bases in dimension 6
statement: Do seven mutually unbiased bases exist in dimension 6?

title: Paley graph clique number
statement: What is the asymptotic clique number of the Paley graph on p vertices?

title: KLS conjecture for log-concave measures
statement: Does the Kannan-Lovasz-Simonovits isoperimetric conjecture hold for all log-concave measures?

title: Sharp graph matrix bounds
statement: Can the norm of random graph matrices be bounded sharply up to 1+epsilon factors?

title: CPNT 1: Irrationality of zeta zero ordinates
statement: Prove that the Riemann zeta function has at least one zero with irrational imaginary part.

title: CPNT 2: Irrationality of first zeta zero
statement: Show that gamma_1 ≈ 14.135 (the first nontrivial zero's ordinate) is irrational.

title: CPNT 3: Zeta derivative at zeros
statement: Obtain upper bounds for 1/|zeta'(rho)| as rho ranges over nontrivial zeros.

title: CPNT 4: Discrete moments of zeta derivative
statement: Compute upper bounds for sum of 1/|zeta'(rho)|^{2k} assuming SZ and GUE.

title: CPNT 6: Zero multiplicity bounds
statement: Improve unconditional upper bounds for multiplicities of nontrivial zeta zeros.

title: CPNT 7: First sign change of prime races
statement: Obtain upper bounds for the first sign change of pi(x;q,a) - pi(x;q,b) in terms of q.

title: CPNT 8: Mertens' theorem for number fields
statement: Localize the first negative value of Delta_K(x) for specific number fields.

title: CPNT 10: Linear independence in function fields
statement: Show that failure of linear independence of L-function zeros always has geometric explanation.

title: CPNT 11: Primes from quadratic forms
statement: Compare prime-counting functions for distinct quadratic forms with identical discriminants.

title: CPNT 13: Prime race ties have density zero
statement: Prove unconditionally that ties in prime races have logarithmic density 0.

title: CPNT 15: Explicit Mertens sum bounds
statement: Find explicit version of M(x) << x exp(-C(log x)^{3/5}(log log x)^{-1/5}).

title: CPNT 17: Sign changes of psi(x)-x
statement: Improve lower bounds for sign changes of the prime-counting error term.

title: UP24 1: Equilibrium support on spheres
statement: Characterize equilibrium measures for Riesz energy with s in (d-3, d) on d-spheres.

title: UP24 2: Dimension reduction of equilibrium measures
statement: When is the support of the equilibrium measure on a manifold full-dimensional vs lower-dimensional?

title: UP24 10: P-frame energy minimizers
statement: For p > 2 and p not an even integer, are all p-frame energy minimizers discrete measures?

title: UP24 12: Phase transitions on the torus
statement: Determine phase transition points between different maximizers on the flat torus.

title: UP24 14: Integration weights for random points
statement: Quantify point quality via positivity of integration weights for random point processes.

title: UP24 15: Snake polynomial expansion
statement: Find classes of majorants whose associated snake polynomials have non-negative Chebyshev expansion.

title: UP24 17: Chromatic numbers of sphere configurations
statement: Find kissing configurations that improve upper bounds for chromatic number of spheres.

title: UP24 18: Explicit approximately Hadamard matrices
statement: Find explicit constructions of approximately Hadamard matrices with small condition ratio.

title: Cap set bounds in Z/4Z and other groups
statement: Generalize exponential cap set bounds from (Z/3Z)^n to (Z/4Z)^n and other groups. Connected to tensor rank.

title: Zaremba's conjecture
statement: Every integer q has a coprime numerator a such that the continued fraction of a/q has bounded partial quotients.

title: McMullen's arithmetic chaos conjecture
statement: Bounded partial quotient sets generate full Hausdorff dimension in the real line.

title: Geometric Bogomolov conjecture (function fields)
statement: Small points on subvarieties of abelian varieties over function fields are dense only on special subvarieties.

title: Kontsevich period conjecture
statement: All algebraic relations between periods come from geometry (the motivic Galois group).

title: Matroid representability over finite fields
statement: Characterize matroids representable over a given finite field. Infinite forbidden minors for GF(5)+.

title: Unique Games Conjecture (fine-grained)
statement: Approximating certain CSPs is NP-hard beyond specific thresholds. Determines the limits of efficient approximation.

title: Kadison-Singer paving (quantitative bounds)
statement: Optimal constants in paving conjectures. Qualitatively solved by Marcus-Spielman-Srivastava 2013, quantitative optimization open.

title: Nodal domain counts on chaotic manifolds
statement: Distribution of nodal domains for high eigenvalues on chaotic Riemannian manifolds. Berry random wave conjecture.

title: Random simplicial complex homology thresholds
statement: Sharp thresholds for vanishing of k-th homology in random d-complexes (Linial-Meshulam model).

title: L-space conjecture
statement: For irreducible rational homology 3-spheres: left-orderable fundamental group iff not an L-space iff admits co-orientable taut foliation.

title: Cluster algebra positivity (general case)
statement: All cluster variables have positive Laurent expansions in terms of any cluster. Known for finite and affine types.

title: Growth gap conjecture
statement: No intermediate growth rates between polynomial and exponential except Grigorchuk-type constructions.

title: KPZ universality in higher dimensions
statement: Characterize universality class of KPZ stochastic PDEs beyond 1D. Hairer's regularity structures handle subcritical but not critical 2D.

title: Morton-Silverman dynamical uniform boundedness
statement: Fix d>=2, D>=1. Exists C(d,D) bounding K-rational preperiodic points of degree-d rational functions over number fields of degree <= D.

title: Sum of square roots problem
statement: Does |sum +-sqrt(a_i)| >= c^n for nonzero sums? Central to complexity of Euclidean shortest paths.

title: Combinatorial Four-Color Theorem proof
statement: Does a proof of the Four-Color Theorem exist using only combinatorial arguments without computer case-checking?

title: Selberg zeta Riemann hypothesis
statement: All non-trivial zeros of the Selberg zeta function of a compact hyperbolic surface lie on Re(s)=1/2.

title: Algebraic Formula Identity Testing (AFIT)
statement: Does a deterministic polynomial-time algorithm exist for deciding if an algebraic formula is identically zero?

title: K3 surface period map image
statement: Give explicit description of which Hodge structures of K3 type arise from algebraic K3 surfaces (image of the period map).

title: Prescribed curvature for affine connections
statement: Given a curvature tensor satisfying Bianchi identity, does an affine connection with that curvature exist on the manifold?

title: Thompson group F amenability
statement: Is the Thompson group F amenable? F is piecewise-linear homeomorphisms of [0,1] with dyadic breakpoints and power-of-2 slopes.

title: Matrix multiplication exponent omega=2
statement: Is the exponent of matrix multiplication exactly 2? Best known: omega < 2.37286 (Alman-Williams 2024). Irreversibility barrier identified.

title: Caccetta-Haggkvist conjecture
statement: Every simple digraph on n vertices with minimum out-degree >= n/k contains a directed cycle of length <= k. Core case k=3 (threshold 1/3) open.
notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 3. Flag algebra bounds plateau at ~0.3465.

title: Sidorenko's conjecture
statement: For every bipartite graph H and graph G with edge density p: hom(H,G) >= p^e(H) * n^v(H). Quasirandom graphs minimize bipartite subgraph density.
notes: Known for trees, complete bipartite, hypercubes. Open for K_{5,5}\C_{10}.

title: Broue's abelian defect group conjecture
statement: For finite group G, p-block B with abelian defect group D: derived categories D^b(B-mod) equivalent to D^b(b-mod) where b is Brauer correspondent.
notes: Proven for cyclic defect, symmetric groups (Chuang-Rouquier 2008). Case-by-case for sporadics.

title: Atiyah conjecture on L2-Betti numbers
statement: For group G with torsion bounded by d, von Neumann dimension of kernel of Q[G]-matrix is in (1/d)Z. Strong version disproved by Austin 2009.
notes: Implies Kaplansky zero-divisor for torsion-free. Proven for elementary amenable with bounded torsion.

title: Greenberg's conjecture on Iwasawa invariants
statement: For totally real K and prime p: lambda = mu = 0 for plus-part of p-adic class group tower in cyclotomic Z_p-extension.
notes: mu=0 proven for abelian K/Q (Ferrero-Washington). lambda=0 verified computationally for many real quadratic fields.

title: Zimmer's conjecture on lattice actions
statement: Irreducible lattice in higher-rank semisimple Lie group acting smoothly on compact manifold of dim < rank: action factors through finite group.
notes: Proven for cocompact lattices in SL(n,R) n>=3 (Brown-Fisher-Hurtado 2020). Extended to SL(n,Z) 2022.

title: Fakhruddin uniform boundedness for rational preimages
statement: Backward orbit tree bounded by B(d,D).
notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. Backward orbit tree bounded by B(d,D). Blocker: TECHNIQUE. Comp: SEARCH.

notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. Characterize tropical curves arising from algebraic curves. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.

statement: Algebraic-logarithmic singularities yield full oscillation-free asymptotic expansions.
notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. Algebraic-logarithmic singularities yield full oscillation-free asymptotic expansions. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.

statement: X_G=X_H implies G isomorphic to H for trees. Verified to 29 vertices.
notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. X_G=X_H implies G isomorphic to H for trees. Verified to 29 vertices. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.

notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. Class group distribution for G-extensions weighted by Aut. Bartel-Lenstra 2020 corrections. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.

title: Selmer group distribution heuristics (BKLPR)

notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. Sel_p distribution over quadratic twists. Implies 50/50/0 for rank 0/1/>=2. p>=3 open. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.

title: Viterbo volume-capacity conjecture
statement: c_1(K)^n <= n!*vol(K) for convex K in R^2n. Symplectic isoperimetric.
notes: Added from Aporia frontier batch 4. c_1(K)^n <= n!*vol(K) for convex K in R^2n. Symplectic isoperimetric. Blocker: TECHNIQUE.
